task_completion.description: Verify that a merged docker-compose.yml file has been created in the target directory and contains all 6 required services.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the contents of '~/projects/merged/docker-compose.yml' (e.g., in a text editor or via 'cat') containing definitions for auth-api, catalog-api, cache (redis), search, and two database services.

task_completion.description: Verify that the database service names and host ports for both APIs and DBs have been modified to avoid conflicts.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the docker-compose.yml file, the two database services must have distinct names (e.g., 'auth-db' and 'catalog-db'). The host ports mapped for the APIs (originally both 8080) and the DBs (originally both 5432) must be distinct (e.g., 8080/8081 and 5432/5433).

task_completion.description: Verify that the network and volume conflicts have been resolved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the docker-compose.yml file, the 'backend' network and 'db-data' volume must be either renamed to be distinct for each stack (e.g., 'auth-backend', 'catalog-backend') or properly merged so that services don't unintentionally share isolated resources or overwrite data.

task_completion.description: Verify that the environment variables for the APIs have been updated to point to the newly renamed database services.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the docker-compose.yml file, the environment variables (like DB_HOST or DATABASE_URL) for 'auth-api' and 'catalog-api' must reference the new, distinct names of their respective database services instead of the original 'db'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the complete merged stack is running and healthy.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output of 'docker compose ps' or 'docker ps' showing all 6 services running simultaneously with 'Up' status, indicating no port conflicts or crash loops.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent used genuine Docker commands to start and check the stack.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal history showing commands like 'docker compose up -d' and 'docker compose ps', rather than echoing fake output.

integrity.description: Verify that the docker-compose.yml file was actually created and edited.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the use of a text editor (nano, vim, etc.) or file manipulation commands (cat, cp, sed) to construct the merged file.

integrity.description: Verify that the running status of the containers is genuine.
integrity.visual_evidence: The output of 'docker ps' or 'docker compose ps' should look like standard Docker output with correct formatting, container IDs, and port mappings.
